text: MiOS v0.2.2 is an immutable, bootc-managed Linux workstation OS distributed as an OCI image at ghcr.io/mios-dev/mios:latest. Base image: ghcr.io/ublue-os/ucore-hci:stable-nvidia (Universal Blue ucore HCI variant — Fedora CoreOS foundation, immutable ostree rootfs, composefs /usr, SELinux enforcing, podman, ZFS, plus uCore additions cockpit/firewalld/tailscale/mergerfs/samba/NFS, plus HCI additions libvirt/KVM/QEMU/VFIO-PCI/virtiofs, plus NVIDIA proprietary akmods MOK-signed, on the LTS Linux 6.12 kernel). MiOS adds GNOME 50, Looking Glass B7, KVM passthrough, k3s, Ceph, a local OpenAI-compatible AI surface, and defense-in-depth hardening. The repo root IS the system root: usr/, etc/, home/, srv/, v1/ mirror the deployed image 1:1.

The Containerfile is single-stage (plus a `ctx` scratch stage that COPYs automation/, usr/, etc/, PACKAGES.md, VERSION, config/artifacts/, tools/ into /ctx). The main FROM is ${BASE_IMAGE} (default ghcr.io/ublue-os/ucore-hci:stable-nvidia). One large RUN block bind-mounts /ctx read-only and a writable /tmp/build copy, sources automation/lib/packages.sh, runs `dnf clean metadata`, `install_packages_strict base`, optionally writes /usr/share/mios/flatpak-list from MIOS_FLATPAKS, runs automation/08-system-files-overlay.sh pre-pipeline, then `CTX=/tmp/build /tmp/build/automation/build.sh` to iterate the numbered phase scripts. Two final RUNs: `ostree container commit`, then `bootc container lint` (LAW 4). No --squash-all — it strips OCI metadata bootc needs.

Build orchestration: Linux uses Justfile (just preflight | build | lint | rechunk | raw | iso | qcow2 | vhdx | wsl2 | sbom | all-bootstrap | flight-status). Windows uses mios-build-local.ps1 (5-phase: prompt for username/password/LUKS/registry → create rootful mios-builder Podman machine with all cores/RAM/250GB → inject creds → podman build + rechunk → BIB disk images → push to GHCR). Other PS1s: preflight.ps1, push-to-github.ps1, Get-MiOS.ps1, install.ps1. Build env vars: MIOS_BASE_IMAGE, MIOS_LOCAL_TAG (localhost/mios:latest), MIOS_USER (mios), MIOS_HOSTNAME (mios), MIOS_FLATPAKS, MIOS_USER_PASSWORD_HASH (required for qcow2/vhdx), MIOS_SSH_PUBKEY.

PACKAGES.md (at usr/share/mios/PACKAGES.md, NOT repo root) is the SSOT for every RPM in the image. Categories live in fenced packages-<category> blocks (e.g. packages-base, packages-critical, packages-self-build). Parsed by automation/lib/packages.sh:get_packages with regex matching the fenced block. Three helpers: install_packages (best-effort, --skip-unavailable), install_packages_strict (fails on any miss), install_packages_optional (never fails). The Containerfile pre-pipeline RUN installs packages-base before automation/build.sh runs. Critical packages are post-validated via rpm -q in automation/build.sh:285-300.

~48 phase scripts at automation/[0-9][0-9]-*.sh, iterated by automation/build.sh in numeric order with `set +e` around each (failures captured in FAIL_LOG/WARN_LOG, never abort the orchestrator). Skipped during build: 08-system-files-overlay.sh (runs pre-pipeline from Containerfile so it never runs twice), 37-ollama-prep.sh (CI-skipped). Sub-phase numbering encodes dependency order. Notable: 01-repos.sh excludes kernel/kernel-core from upgrades (lines 65,68), 19-k3s-selinux.sh ships SELinux modules without auto-loading, 34-gpu-detect.sh writes /run/mios/gpu-passthrough.status, 37-selinux.sh declares semanage booleans/fcontexts, 52-bake-kvmfr.sh bakes KVMFR, 53-bake-lookingglass-client.sh bakes Looking Glass B7, 90-generate-sbom.sh runs syft.

Kernel arguments are TOML files under usr/lib/bootc/kargs.d/, top entry 00-mios.toml. Format is a flat `kargs = ["key=value", ...]` array at top level — NO [kargs] section header, NO `delete` sub-key. bootc container lint rejects anything else; files processed lexicographically; later files cannot remove earlier kargs in the same image (use runtime `bootc kargs --delete`). MiOS hardening kargs include slab_nomerge, randomize_kstack_offset=on, pti=on, vsyscall=none, lockdown=integrity (NOT confidentiality), spectre_v2=on, spec_store_bypass_disable=on, l1tf=full,force, gather_data_sampling=force, plus iommu=pt, amd_iommu=on/intel_iommu=on, nvidia-drm.modeset=1. init_on_alloc=1, init_on_free=1, page_alloc.shuffle=1 are explicitly DISABLED in MiOS due to NVIDIA/CUDA memory-init incompatibility — re-enable only on CPU-only builds via a higher-priority kargs.d file.

Six Architectural Laws (INDEX.md §3, all enforced/non-negotiable): 1) USR-OVER-ETC — static config under /usr/lib/<component>.d/, /etc/ is admin-override only (exceptions: /etc/yum.repos.d/, /etc/nvidia-container-toolkit/). 2) NO-MKDIR-IN-VAR — every /var/ path declared via usr/lib/tmpfiles.d/*.conf. 3) BOUND-IMAGES — every Quadlet image symlinked into /usr/lib/bootc/bound-images.d/ (binder loop in automation/08-system-files-overlay.sh:74-86). 4) BOOTC-CONTAINER-LINT — final RUN of Containerfile. 5) UNIFIED-AI-REDIRECTS — MIOS_AI_KEY/MODEL/ENDPOINT all resolve to http://localhost:8080/v1; vendor URLs are forbidden anywhere in the image. 6) UNPRIVILEGED-QUADLETS — every Quadlet declares User=, Group=, Delegate=yes (documented exceptions: mios-ceph, mios-k3s as User=root because Ceph/K3s require uid 0).

Local AI surface (LAW 5). All MiOS agents target the OpenAI-compatible endpoint at http://localhost:8080/v1, served by the LocalAI Quadlet at etc/containers/systemd/mios-ai.container. Surfaces: GET /v1/models, POST /v1/chat/completions (SSE streaming, tool calling for capable models), POST /v1/embeddings. Auth: Authorization: Bearer $MIOS_AI_KEY (empty key accepted locally). Discovery: usr/share/mios/ai/v1/models.json (catalog), usr/share/mios/ai/v1/mcp.json (MCP server registry). Canonical agent prompt: usr/share/mios/ai/system.md. Override chain: ~/.config/mios/system-prompt.md → /etc/mios/ai/system-prompt.md → /usr/share/mios/ai/system.md.

Pipeline phases (INDEX.md §6) span two repos: Phase-0 preflight + profile load + identity capture (mios-bootstrap.git/install.sh), Phase-1 Total Root Merge — clone mios.git and mios-bootstrap.git into / and overlay (mios-bootstrap.git/install.sh), Phase-2 build the running system — Containerfile + automation/[0-9][0-9]-*.sh sub-phases, OR dnf install on FHS (mios.git), Phase-3 systemd-sysusers/tmpfiles/daemon-reload + user create + per-user ~/.config/mios/{profile.toml,system-prompt.md} staging (both repos), Phase-4 reboot prompt (mios-bootstrap.git/install.sh).

Composefs enabled via usr/lib/ostree/prepare-root.conf: [composefs] enabled=true, [etc] transient=true, [root] transient-ro=true. Hardware acceleration uses universal CDI (Container Device Interface) for NVIDIA, AMD ROCm/KFD, Intel iGPU; specs to /var/run/cdi/, admin overrides /etc/cdi/, declared in usr/lib/tmpfiles.d/mios-gpu.conf. GPU passthrough targets detected at runtime by automation/34-gpu-detect.sh writing /run/mios/gpu-passthrough.status. KVMFR shared-memory and Looking Glass B7 baked in-image (automation/52-bake-kvmfr.sh, automation/53-bake-lookingglass-client.sh).

Defense-in-depth security stack. SELinux enforcing with five custom modules in usr/share/selinux/packages/mios/ (mios_portabled, mios_kvmfr, mios_cdi, mios_quadlet, mios_sysext) — compiled and shipped, not auto-loaded at build. Booleans: container_use_cephfs, virt_use_samba. Fcontext: /var/home(/.*)? → user_home_dir_t. firewalld default-deny zone=drop, allowed: cockpit/9090, ssh/22, libvirt bridge, CrowdSec nftables bouncer (automation/33-firewall.sh). CrowdSec sovereign/offline mode (automation/12-virt.sh:42-50 disables online_client). fapolicyd deny-by-default with rules at etc/fapolicyd/fapolicyd.rules. USBGuard installed off-by-default. Image signing via cosign keyless from .github/workflows/mios-ci.yml on every tag and main push.

Deployment via BIB (bootc-image-builder, quay.io/centos-bootc/bootc-image-builder:latest), per-format TOMLs in config/artifacts/: just raw → 80GiB ext4 RAW (bib.toml), just iso → Anaconda installer ISO (iso.toml — only mount this, mounting both bib.toml and iso.toml crashes BIB with 'found config.json and also config.toml'), just qcow2 → QEMU qcow2 (qcow2.toml, requires MIOS_USER_PASSWORD_HASH), just vhdx → BIB emits .vhd VPC then qemu-img converts to .vhdx (vhdx.toml, requires MIOS_USER_PASSWORD_HASH), just wsl2 → tar.gz for `wsl --import` (wsl2.toml). Day-2: bootc upgrade && systemctl reboot, bootc switch <ref>, bootc rollback. End-user install Linux: `sudo bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mios-dev/mios-bootstrap/main/install.sh)"`. Windows: `irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mios-dev/mios-bootstrap/main/install.ps1 | iex`.

Self-build chain: MiOS vN (running) → podman build → MiOS vN+1 (OCI image) → bootc-base-imagectl rechunk --max-layers 67 (from hhd-dev/rechunk, 5-10x smaller Day-2 deltas) → cosign keyless sign → push to GHCR → bootc upgrade → reboot. Five build modes: Mode 0 bootstrap (curl install.sh), Mode 1 CI/CD (.github/workflows/mios-ci.yml on tag and main), Mode 2 Windows (mios-build-local.ps1), Mode 3 Linux just, Mode 4 self-build from running MiOS, Mode 5 Ignition appliance (config/ignition/ Butane configs compiled with butane to .ign). image-builder-cli (osbuild/image-builder-cli) is under evaluation as BIB successor.

Shell and Containerfile invariants enforced in CI (.github/workflows/mios-ci.yml). Shell: `set -euo pipefail` at the top of every phase script. `VAR=$((VAR + 1))` only — `((VAR++))` is forbidden because under `set -e` it exits 1 when the result is 0. shellcheck-clean; SC2038 fatal. File names: NN-name.sh where NN encodes execution order. Containerfile: ctx scratch stage holds context, main stage bind-mounts /ctx read-only with mutable copy at /tmp/build, final RUN is bootc container lint, no --squash-all, kernel rule restricts to kernel-modules-extra/devel/headers/tools (never kernel/kernel-core; excluded in automation/01-repos.sh:65,68), dnf flag is install_weak_deps=False (underscore — dnf5; install_weakdeps without underscore is dnf4 and silently ignored).

Override surfaces (SECURITY.md). Kernel kargs: `bootc kargs edit` (runtime), or higher-priority usr/lib/bootc/kargs.d/*.toml (image-time). Sysctl: /etc/sysctl.d/ files override /usr/lib/sysctl.d/99-mios-hardening.conf. SELinux: setenforce 0 (transient), /etc/selinux/config (persistent). Firewall: firewall-cmd --add-service=... or --add-port=.... CrowdSec: cscli decisions delete --all. fapolicyd: /etc/fapolicyd/fapolicyd.trust. USBGuard: /etc/usbguard/rules.conf or `usbguard allow-device <id>`. AI prompt: ~/.config/mios/system-prompt.md (user) → /etc/mios/ai/system-prompt.md (host) → /usr/share/mios/ai/system.md (vendor canonical). Verification: `bootc status`, `systemctl --failed`, `mios "what is the current image tag?"`, `firewall-cmd --list-all`, `getenforce` (must be Enforcing).

Profile and env three-layer overlay (INDEX.md §4). Profile (TOML): ~/.config/mios/profile.toml (per-user, seeded into uid≥1000 homes from /etc/skel) → /etc/mios/profile.toml (host/admin) → /usr/share/mios/profile.toml (vendor defaults, immutable). Env (sh, resolved by /etc/profile.d/mios-env.sh at login): ~/.config/mios/env → /etc/mios/install.env → /etc/mios/env.d/*.env → /usr/share/mios/env.defaults → ~/.env.mios (legacy). Higher layers supersede lower field-by-field. Defaults policy: every boolean ships true; never disable a component via static config. systemd Condition* directives short-circuit incompatible components at boot.

Cosign keyless verification command (verbatim from SECURITY.md and DEPLOY.md): `cosign verify --certificate-identity-regexp="https://github.com/mios-dev/mios" --certificate-oidc-issuer="https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com" ghcr.io/mios-dev/mios:latest`. The .github/workflows/mios-ci.yml workflow keyless-signs every tag and main push. SLSA provenance + SPDX SBOM attestations available via `cosign verify-attestation --type slsaprovenance|spdxjson|cyclonedx ghcr.io/mios-dev/mios:latest`.

bootc is a CNCF Sandbox project that boots and upgrades a Linux host from an OCI container image. The booted host is backed by ostree (today) with composefs work in progress as bootc composefs-native. Key commands: `bootc status [--format=json]`, `bootc upgrade [--apply]`, `bootc switch <imgref>`, `bootc rollback`, `bootc kargs edit`, `bootc kargs --append`, `bootc kargs --delete`, `bootc install to-disk`, `bootc install to-filesystem`, `bootc container lint`. Install commands must run inside the privileged container being installed.

bootc container lint enforces (LAW 4): kernel present at /usr/lib/modules/<kver>/vmlinuz; no files written under /var or /run in image layers; /usr structurally valid (no dangling symlinks, no unexpected setuid); OCI config has architecture and os fields; /sbin/init is systemd PID 1; kargs.d files use the flat kargs = [...] TOML format only. As of bootc 1.5+, bootc upgrade does a pre-flight free-space check (#1995); if /sysroot is full, `bootc rollback` (clear staged) or `ostree admin undeploy <index>` (drop a pinned old deployment) frees space.

ostree is a content-addressed object store and deployment manager — like git for filesystem trees. Each commit references a tree of file objects deduplicated by SHA-256. A deployment is a hardlinked checkout of a commit at /sysroot/ostree/deploy/<stateroot>/deploy/<csum>.<n>. bootc currently uses ostree as backend; rpm-ostree and bootc interoperate via shared kargs metadata and ostree refs under ostree/container/image/<digest>. Key concepts: refs, deployments, /sysroot physical root, /var mutable subvolume (LAW 2), /etc 3-way merge on every bootc upgrade.

composefs combines overlayfs + EROFS + fs-verity to produce a verifiable read-only mount. mkcomposefs synthesizes an EROFS image whose metacopy xattrs point at backing files; mount.composefs validates each backing file's fs-verity digest before exposing it. Tampering produces EIO, not silent corruption. Composefs 1.0 stabilized the on-disk format. MiOS enables it via usr/lib/ostree/prepare-root.conf with [composefs] enabled=true, [etc] transient=true (fresh tmpfs each boot, populated from image + 3-way merge), [root] transient-ro=true (root mount is read-only, runtime writes go to /var).

ucore (ublue-os/ucore) is an OCI base image of Fedora CoreOS with batteries included. Multi-arch (linux/amd64 + linux/arm64) since 2025-11-08. Tags: :stable, :testing, :stable-nvidia, :stable-nvidia-lts. ZFS in base since 2025-06-12 (older :*-zfs variants deprecated). ucore-hci is the hyper-converged-infra spin used by MiOS as base — adds libvirt/KVM, QEMU, VFIO-PCI, virtiofs on top of ucore. The :stable-nvidia tag includes proprietary akmod-built MOK-signed NVIDIA drivers and the NVIDIA Container Toolkit. Sibling images: Bluefin (GNOME dev), Aurora (KDE), Bazzite (gaming/handheld).

LocalAI (github.com/mudler/LocalAI, MIT-licensed) is the OpenAI-API-compatible facade with the broadest backend support: llama.cpp, vLLM-style, transformers, gpt4all, exllama, whisper.cpp, stable-diffusion, embeddings, audio TTS/STT — all behind one consistent /v1/* surface. MiOS treats it as the runtime; users swap models via the LocalAI manifest, not by swapping endpoint code. Surfaces at http://localhost:8080/v1: GET /v1/models, POST /v1/chat/completions (SSE + tools), POST /v1/embeddings. Auth: Authorization: Bearer $MIOS_AI_KEY (empty key accepted). Tool calling supported by Llama 3.1+, Qwen 2.5+, Hermes 3+, Firefunction, Mistral 0.3+ — strict mode accepted but not enforced; ship schemas as in-context documentation.

CDI (Container Device Interface, github.com/cncf-tags/container-device-interface) is the vendor-agnostic CNCF-tag spec for declaring what a device exposes (kernel modules, device nodes, libs to bind-mount, env vars). Replaces per-vendor OCI hooks. MiOS detection: automation/34-gpu-detect.sh writes /run/mios/gpu-passthrough.status; a systemd one-shot calls `nvidia-ctk cdi generate --output=/var/run/cdi/nvidia.yaml`. Admin overrides land in /etc/cdi/. usr/lib/tmpfiles.d/mios-gpu.conf ensures the directories exist on every boot (LAW 2). Use: `podman run --device nvidia.com/gpu=all`, `podman run --device amd.com/gpu=all`, `podman run --device intel.com/gpu=card0`.

dnf5 is the C++ rewrite of dnf with a libdnf5 daemon and faster resolver. Critical knob for MiOS image builds: install_weak_deps=False (underscore — dnf5 spelling). The dnf4 form install_weakdeps (no underscore) is silently ignored by dnf5 — this caused real bugs. MiOS uses BuildKit cache mounts: --mount=type=cache,dst=/var/cache/libdnf5,sharing=locked and --mount=type=cache,dst=/var/cache/dnf,sharing=locked — cache mounts aren't baked into the image, so 5-10x faster rebuilds without layer bloat. Kernel rule: only kernel-modules-extra/devel/headers/tools may be installed; never kernel or kernel-core (excluded in automation/01-repos.sh:65,68 — base image owns the kernel; upgrading desyncs ABI from akmod-built NVIDIA modules).

bootc-image-builder (BIB) is the osbuild-based tool that converts a bootc OCI image into a deployable disk image. Image: quay.io/centos-bootc/bootc-image-builder:latest (Justfile:14). Output types: ami, anaconda-iso, gce, iso, qcow2, raw, vhd, vmdk, wsl2. Run as a privileged podman container with -v ./output:/output -v /var/lib/containers/storage:/var/lib/containers/storage. Configuration TOML at ./config.toml with sections [customizations.installer.kickstart], [customizations.iso], [customizations.user], [customizations.kernel]. Note: [customizations.user] and [customizations.installer.kickstart] are mutually exclusive. CRITICAL gotcha: the iso recipe must mount ONLY iso.toml; mounting both bib.toml and iso.toml crashes BIB with 'found config.json and also config.toml'.

Cosign keyless flow: ephemeral keypair (in-memory) → OIDC token (GitHub Actions: token.actions.githubusercontent.com) → Fulcio CA issues short-lived (~10 min) X.509 cert binding identity → image digest signed → entry uploaded to Rekor transparency log → signature pushed to OCI registry as <digest>.sig companion artifact. No long-lived signing key ever touches disk; identity is the GitHub Actions workflow itself. Verify MiOS: `cosign verify --certificate-identity-regexp='https://github.com/mios-dev/mios' --certificate-oidc-issuer='https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com' ghcr.io/mios-dev/mios:latest`. Attestation predicate types: slsaprovenance, slsaprovenance1, spdxjson, cyclonedx, vuln, openvex.

NVIDIA on Fedora bootc uses akmods + nvidia-container-toolkit. MiOS uses :stable-nvidia (open kernel modules, Turing+) from ucore-hci; for older GPUs (Maxwell/Pascal) override MIOS_BASE_IMAGE to ghcr.io/ublue-os/ucore-hci:stable-nvidia-lts (proprietary 580 LTS). Kernel module signing is mandatory because the Fedora kernel is locked-down (lockdown=integrity); akmods builds the modules at base-image build time and signs them with the MOK chain. Containers see the GPU via the CDI spec generated by `nvidia-ctk cdi generate`. nvidia-drm.modeset=1 karg required for GNOME Wayland. NOTE: init_on_alloc=1, init_on_free=1, page_alloc.shuffle=1 are explicitly DISABLED in MiOS due to NVIDIA/CUDA memory-init incompatibility.

On Hyper-V Gen2 VMs the hyperv_fb framebuffer combined with Plymouth's animations causes a visible boot hang and occasional video reset. MiOS workaround: usr/lib/bootc/kargs.d/05-mios-plymouth.toml containing `kargs = ["plymouth.enable=0", "rd.plymouth=0"]` with `match-architectures = ["x86_64"]`. Files in usr/lib/bootc/kargs.d/ use a flat top-level kargs array — no [kargs] section header, no delete sub-key, no nested tables. bootc container lint rejects malformed TOML.

MiOS deployment targets via just/BIB: Hyper-V Gen2 VHDX (just vhdx — BIB emits .vhd VPC, qemu-img convert to .vhdx, attach to Gen 2 VM with secure boot Microsoft UEFI CA template), QEMU/libvirt qcow2 (just qcow2; virt-install --import), bare-metal via anaconda-iso (just iso), WSL2 via tar export (just wsl2; wsl --import MiOS C:\WSL\MiOS output/disk.wsl2). qcow2/vhdx require MIOS_USER_PASSWORD_HASH=$(openssl passwd -6 'pass') and optional MIOS_SSH_PUBKEY. WSL2 ignores image kargs (Windows-hosted kernel); /etc/wsl.conf has systemd=true.

VFIO GPU passthrough on MiOS requires kargs intel_iommu=on (or amd_iommu=on), iommu=pt, vfio-pci.ids=<vendor:device>. MiOS detects passthrough-eligible devices at runtime via automation/34-gpu-detect.sh writing /run/mios/gpu-passthrough.status. Bind via etc/modprobe.d/vfio.conf with `options vfio-pci ids=...` plus `softdep nvidia pre: vfio-pci` and `softdep nouveau pre: vfio-pci`. CPU isolation for VM workloads uses isolcpus=2-7 nohz_full=2-7 rcu_nocbs=2-7 — drop in a dedicated higher-priority kargs.d file (e.g. 50-mios-cpu-isolation.toml) so non-passthrough hosts can override with 99-disable-isolation.toml. Looking Glass B7 + KVMFR (baked via automation/52-bake-kvmfr.sh and 53-bake-lookingglass-client.sh) provide low-latency display return.

K3s on MiOS runs as a single-node workstation cluster (mios-k3s.container Quadlet). Documented exception to LAW 6 (UNPRIVILEGED-QUADLETS): User=root because K3s requires uid 0. Service gating: ConditionVirtualization=!wsl,!container (skips on WSL2 and inside containers — INDEX.md §5). SELinux integration: automation/19-k3s-selinux.sh ships custom policy (compiled, not auto-loaded). Cockpit's kubernetes module surfaces the cluster in a browser. cephadm referenced as path to multi-node storage but mios-ceph.container also documented LAW 6 exception (User=root, Ceph requires uid 0); single-node MiOS workstations typically use BlueStore on a dedicated disk.

MiOS kernel hardening (usr/lib/bootc/kargs.d/00-mios.toml, sourced from SecureBlue audit framework + Fedora hardening guidelines): slab_nomerge, randomize_kstack_offset=on, pti=on, vsyscall=none, lockdown=integrity (MiOS chose integrity over confidentiality to allow kexec for in-image testing), spectre_v2=on, spec_store_bypass_disable=on, l1tf=full,force, gather_data_sampling=force, plus iommu=pt + amd_iommu=on/intel_iommu=on for VFIO, plus nvidia-drm.modeset=1 for GNOME Wayland. DISABLED for NVIDIA compatibility: init_on_alloc=1, init_on_free=1, page_alloc.shuffle=1 (re-enable on CPU-only builds via higher-priority kargs.d file). FIPS: opt-in via 01-mios-fips.toml with `kargs = ["fips=1"]` plus `update-crypto-policies --no-reload --set FIPS` baked at build time.

MiOS sysctl hardening (usr/lib/sysctl.d/99-mios-hardening.conf): kernel.kptr_restrict=2, kernel.dmesg_restrict=1, kernel.perf_event_paranoid=3, kernel.sysrq=0, kernel.yama.ptrace_scope=2, kernel.unprivileged_bpf_disabled=1, net.core.bpf_jit_harden=2, kernel.kexec_load_disabled=1, kernel.io_uring_disabled=2. Network: net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies=1, net.ipv4.conf.all.{accept_redirects=0,send_redirects=0,rp_filter=1,accept_source_route=0,log_martians=1}, net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts=1, net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps=0. Filesystem: fs.suid_dumpable=0, fs.protected_{hardlinks,symlinks}=1, fs.protected_{fifos,regular}=2. Admin overrides go in /etc/sysctl.d/.

MiOS CI lint stack (.github/workflows/mios-ci.yml): hadolint on Containerfile, shellcheck on every numbered script (SC2038 fatal), TOML lint (taplo) on every .toml (kargs.d, iso.toml, install configs), `bootc container lint` after build (LAW 4), and `cosign verify` on the produced image before pushing :latest. Renovate keeps base-image digests (image-versions.yml), GitHub Actions, and Rust crates current; auto-merge gated on green CI.

Sibling Universal Blue images: Bluefin (developer workstation, GNOME, devcontainer focus), Aurora (KDE), Bazzite (gaming/handheld). MiOS sits closest to ucore/ucore-hci with a workstation-server hybrid posture. Other immutable distros for comparison: Fedora Silverblue/Kinoite (rpm-ostree, pre-bootc), CoreOS Layering (rpm-ostree predecessor), RHEL bootc (closed source, requires subscription), CentOS Stream bootc (where BIB is published from), NixOS (declarative, not OCI), Talos (Kubernetes-only, API-driven, no SSH/shell), Flatcar (Container Linux successor, server-only), Vanilla OS (Ubuntu-based dual-root atomic), openSUSE MicroOS (btrfs snapshots).

MiOS local AI override chain. Three layers, highest precedence first: ~/.config/mios/system-prompt.md (per-user) → /etc/mios/ai/system-prompt.md (host/admin) → /usr/share/mios/ai/system.md (vendor canonical, immutable). The agent loads vendor first, then merges host, then user. CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md at the repo root are per-tool stubs that just point at this chain (CLAUDE.md adds Claude-specific deltas: cwd=/ is repo and system root, confirm before git push/bootc upgrade/dnf install/systemctl/rm -rf, deliverables are complete replacement files). Memory: /var/lib/mios/ai/memory/. Scratch: /var/lib/mios/ai/scratch/.

OpenAI vector_stores chunking contract: auto strategy defaults to max_chunk_size_tokens=800, chunk_overlap_tokens=400; static strategy allows max_chunk_size_tokens in [100,4096] and chunk_overlap_tokens ≤ max/2. Per-file size limit 512 MB; vector store ≤ 10,000 files (≤ 100,000,000 for stores created from Nov 2025). Attributes ≤ 16 keys, key ≤ 64 chars, value ≤ 512 chars / number / boolean. Storage cost: first GB free then $0.10/GB/day. Client moved from client.beta.vector_stores to client.vector_stores — older code samples may show the beta path.

OpenAI strict-mode function calling requires three things: strict: true, additionalProperties: false on every object, every property listed in required. Optional fields are modeled by allowing null, e.g. {"type": ["string", "null"]}. Without these, the request is rejected by OpenAI cloud. Local runtimes (LocalAI, Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio, llama.cpp) accept strict: true as an unknown field and proceed without enforcement — schema still useful as in-context documentation but runtime does not reject malformed JSON. For enforced JSON Schema compliance locally, use vLLM with xgrammar or llama.cpp grammars (compile JSON Schema to GBNF).

Day-0 local-model compatibility for the MiOS KB. The MiOS host's own LocalAI endpoint at http://localhost:8080/v1 is the canonical local target — the same endpoint MiOS's own system agents use, satisfying LAW 5. Other compatible runtimes: Ollama (default localhost:11434/v1), vLLM (vllm serve <model> exposes localhost:8000/v1), LM Studio (localhost:1234/v1), llama.cpp server (./llama-server), LiteLLM proxy (translates many backends including Responses↔Chat Completions, localhost:4000/v1 typical), OpenRouter cloud aggregator (openrouter.ai/api/v1). All accept the same Chat Completions shape. Set MIOS_AI_ENDPOINT to point at any of them; the rest of the KB (chunks.jsonl, sft.jsonl, dpo.jsonl, eval dataset, chat.local.example.json) works unchanged.
